title: Seventy Times Seven
subtitle: Christ teaches how to win back the brother who has sinned, and how often to forgive

stillness: Be still before the Lord, who rejoices over one sinner converted.
You also have a brother who strays.
Quiet your heart, and listen to how He would have you win him back.
gospel: If your brother shall sin against you,
go, and correct him between you and him alone.
If he shall hear you, you shall gain your brother.
But if he will not hear you,
take with you one or two more,
that in the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may stand.
And if he will not hear them, tell the Church.
And if he will not hear the Church,
let him be to you as the heathen and the publican.
behold: If you alone know it — not as God through confession, but as a man through certain knowledge —
you must correct him in a friendly way, secretly, and apart.
Warn him sweetly and lovingly, between you and him alone,
so that what is hidden is not dragged into the open.
Be a mender, not a betrayer of the crime;
intend his correction, and spare his shame.
do not praise him, by flattering;
do not slay him, by reviling;
do not keep silent, out of bashfulness;
do not neglect him, out of sloth.
consider: To correct the one who sins is a command laid upon all, toward all.
For he who sees his brother sin and is silent sins as much as he who shows no mercy to the sinner.
This correction is ordered to the mending of the sinning brother;
therefore it is an act of charity.
Lest, if once publicly corrected he lose his shame, he remain in his sin,
and grown more stubborn, stay scarcely to be mended;
for out of shame he may begin to defend his sin,
and the one you wish to make better, you make worse.
Hear Seneca: if you bear a friend's vices, you make them your own;
you sin twice over, when you lend your compliance to his sin.
If he hears you — repenting and ceasing from sin — you have gained your brother, saving his soul;
and his calling back turns to your own spiritual gain, the salvation of your soul,
for, as Jerome says, through another's salvation, salvation is won for us also.
The first correction is of love; the second, of fear; the third, of shame.
First the brother is to be called back by love;
and if this does not avail, called back by fear;
and if this does not suffice, called back by shame.
And those whom you cannot convert by correcting or warning or accusing,
strive at least to win by praying.
prayer: grant me, unworthy as I am, in Your mercy,
that I may mend my sinning brother, correcting him with words in due season;
and if not by words, then at least by prayers and good examples, may I not cease to do this.
Grant me also that, abiding in the unity of the Church and in fraternal charity,
I may deserve to be heard in my prayers,
and to know You by Your favor and help in our midst,
You who have set Your dwelling among the peaceable.
that as often as my brother shall sin against me, so often I may forgive him,
so that neither rancor within, nor any sign of rancor without, may be found in me in any way.

latinJewel.latin: Quos autem non potes corripiendo, aut monendo vel accusando convertere, studeas saltem orando acquirere.
latinJewel.english: But those whom you cannot convert by correcting, or by warning, or by accusing, strive at least to win by praying.
